title: OSCAR: Omni-Embodiment Action-Conditioned World Model for Robotics
summary: OSCAR is a video world model for robotics designed to generalize across diverse robot embodiments by unifying action conditioning via 2D kinematic-skeleton rendering as a shared representation. Built by fine-tuning the Cosmos-Predict2.5-2B model on a large-scale standardized data pipeline, it improves action-following fidelity and motion quality while enabling virtual policy evaluation without physical hardware. The approach addresses the fragmentation of embodiment-specific models by providing a single omni-embodiment framework compatible with multiple robot morphologies.
